user_message: How should I coach Tom Rodriguez on Iron Creek Mining? I met with them last week but can't remember the details.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Karen Berg <karen.berg@ironcreekmin.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-13 12:05 EST
Subject: Follow-up: account health Review

Our team encountered some challenges with account health. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

---
From: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
To: Karen Berg <karen.berg@ironcreekmin.com>
Date: 2024-01-20 14:50 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Iron Creek Mining account health

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about account health. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

Meeting: account health Discussion - Iron Creek Mining
Date: 2024-02-20
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Karen Berg (Iron Creek Mining), Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft)

[00:01:15] Karen Berg (Iron Creek Mining): Appreciate everyone making time. The main agenda today is account health.

[00:06:00] Karen Berg (Iron Creek Mining): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is stakeholder alignment.

[00:11:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is timeline constraints.

[00:16:00] Karen Berg (Iron Creek Mining): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is integration complexity.

[00:18:00] Karen Berg (Iron Creek Mining): On the account health front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around timeline constraints.

[00:23:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): On the account health front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around vendor dependencies.

[00:27:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on technical validation and found that the cost projections need to factor in reserved instance pricing for accuracy.

[00:30:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on budget approval and found that there's a compliance requirement we hadn't accounted for in the original scope.

[00:33:00] Karen Berg (Iron Creek Mining): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is security compliance.

[00:36:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): On the account health front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around stakeholder alignment.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Complete, well-structured output matching the Pre-Meeting Brief template with all required artifacts present and actionable
Score 3: Output covers main requirements but missing one or two secondary artifacts or lacking specificity
Score 1: Output is generic, missing key artifacts, or fails to address the specific request
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct account health decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
